late_news.headline: Marsch 'had to beg' U.S. players to sing anthem
late_news.detail: Former United States men's national team (USMNT) assistant coach Jesse March has revealed he sometimes "had to beg" the American players to sing the national anthem.

late_news.detail: USMNT coach Mauricio Pochettino said that the time for fiery speeches is over and that his side is ready for Friday's World Cup opener against Paraguay.

late_news.headline: Palestine FA boss: U.S. denied my WCup visa
late_news.detail: The head of the Palestinian Football Association is waiting in Mexico City for permission to enter the United States with other federation heads attending the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

late_news.detail: Brazil are the most successful team in World Cup history. But after not winning the title in 24 years, can Carlo Ancelotti's side finally end that run?

late_news.detail: USMNT striker Folarin Balogun, who could have played for England or Nigeria, said his two goals in Friday's 4-1 World Cup victory over Paraguay made for a "dreamy night."

late_news.headline: Dempsey slams Marsch over anthem: Stay in lane
late_news.detail: United States legend Clint Dempsey had some choice words for former national team assistant Jesse Marsch, who suggested Thursday that some American players needed encouragement to sing the anthem ahead of games.
